_comment: Per-project config consumed by the agent-loop skills (research-run, qa-run, implementer-run, reviewer-run, validator-run, unblock, investigate-run, stale-sweep). Save this filled-in as ~/.claude/agent-loop/<project>/config.json. The skills read $1 (project arg) or fall back to the name in ~/.claude/agent-loop/default.txt.

linear._comment: Set use=false if this project doesn't use Linear (e.g. side projects with just GitHub Issues). Skills will degrade gracefully.
linear._team_id_comment: The team's UUID. The LINEAR BINDING block in every skill confirms it's authed to the right workspace by checking list_teams contains this id. Get it once via mcp__linear-server__list_teams. Required when linear.use=true.

label_ids._comment: Optional. If omitted, skills look up by name on first run and cache. Looking up by ID is cheaper.

agent_backlog_project._comment: Optional dedicated project for agent-generated tickets, keeps main backlog clean. Set name/id to '' to file directly into the team backlog.
agent_backlog_project.name: Agent Backlog

releaser._comment: releaser-run. default_scope: 'agent-only' (default — only auto-release commits whose PR closed an agent-labeled agent-done ticket; never auto-ship your own merges) or 'all'. Per-repo release.scope overrides. Directed releases (/releaser-run <project> --release <repo>[@<ref>], or a [release-request] ticket) bypass this and ship what you name, through all gates.

dev_verify._comment: dev-verify-run: post-deploy verifier. After a merged PR is confirmed LIVE on dev (repos[].health.version_url/dev_url, else settle_delay), runs the test-flow flows for the changed capabilities against dev and writes failures to findings_ledger -> manager `verify` bucket. Reuses validator's diff->capability planning + qa's flow-runner. Omit if you don't run dev-verify-run.

coverage._comment: coverage-run. Finds capability×surface cells with no flow and opens grounded flow PRs to the test-flow repo (paced). Uses qa.test_flow_repo + researcher.architecture_repo (capability docs) + the api-client types for grounding.

manager._comment: manager-run intake/grooming — the single paced gate from findings sources into the issue tracker. PER-SOURCE BUCKETS: each source gets its own Linear label (its bucket) and its own depth, so audit / qa / research never starve each other. Each sources[] entry: {name, findings_json, format(audit-v1|qa-v1|research-v1), label(default=name; audit defaults to audit_label), target_depth(default below), investigate_wip(default below), report_md(optional)}. target_queue_depth/investigate_wip below are the FALLBACK per-source defaults. risky_categories_regex routes critical/high + security/auth/money findings to investigate-first.

backend._comment: Resilience layer (see references/LINEAR-ACCESS.md). mode='linear' (default): Linear is the source of truth, skills detect whichever Linear MCP binding is live (mcp__linear-server__* OR mcp__claude_ai_Linear__*) and degrade to a read-only board cache when none is. mode='local' (a full machine-local board, no Linear) is reserved/not built.

slack._comment: All optional. Empty webhook URL = silently skip notifications (the run never fails on missing Slack).

health._comment: ops-run watches these. dev_url/prod_url GET-probed (expect 2xx). critical_routes appended to the base host (expect non-5xx). Omit the block on repos with no running service.

release._comment: releaser-run. autonomy: off|prepare|dev|full. 'full' (auto dev->prod+rollback) requires smoke AND rollback set, else it downgrades to dev. mechanism is a class hint; the repo's own CLAUDE.md deploy section is the source of truth.

_repos_contributor_skill_note: Optional per-repo `contributor_skill`: the name of a Claude skill carrying repo-specific conventions (test commands, architecture, migration patterns). The implementer invokes it before writing code in that repo. If omitted, the implementer falls back to scanning available skills by repo name.

qa._comment: Which repo holds the test-flow markdown contracts. qa-run records findings to `findings_ledger` (default ~/.claude/agent-loop/<project>/findings/qa-findings.json) instead of filing Linear tickets — /manager-run tickets them paced. Omit the whole `qa` block if this project doesn't run qa-run.

researcher._comment: research-run no longer files tickets directly — it records findings to `findings_ledger` (default ~/.claude/agent-loop/<project>/findings/research-findings.json) which /manager-run reads and paces into the tracker. Add the same path as a `research` source in manager.sources[]. architecture_repo/path enable the optional `architecture` research mode (dropped if empty).

loop._comment: Pacing for /loop dynamic mode. Skills use these defaults if config omits them.
